sub: Build a complete Java application using every tool
contents: The Capstone Project Requirements Checklist AI Usage Rules Architecture Checklist (Bonus) Exercise 10.1: Project Setup Exercise 10.2: Implementation + Tests Exercise 10.3: Quality + Pipeline LLM Bonus Criteria Evaluation Criteria

title: The Capstone Project
desc: Build a complete Java application using every tool from this course. Solo project — submitted via GitHub.

body.card: Project: A command-line Student Grade Manager
items: Add students and their grades
Calculate averages, min, max
Save/load data from JSON files
Input validation and error handling

body.quote: Complete cognitive-core setup (Module 9) before starting. AI-augmented throughout with Claude Code as your co-pilot.

title: Requirements Checklist
desc: Each requirement maps to a module. Grading rules (penalties & bonuses): see Module 11 — Grading Policy.

rows: Code in Git with meaningful commits

body.card: You MAY use Claude Code for:
items: Generating boilerplate (pom.xml, project structure)
Writing commit messages
Generating test cases (but review them!)
Debugging build and pipeline errors
Code review before committing
Design decisions (class structure, method signatures)
Explain every line of code in your project
Fix at least 3 quality violations by hand
Write at least 3 test cases by hand

rows: Git log tells a clear story
table.note: Bonus points for students who complete this architecture evaluation.

title: Project Plan: 3 Phases
desc: Each phase ends with a verification command that must succeed before you move on.

items: Create public GitHub repo: student-grade-manager
Use Claude Code to scaffold Maven project
Add dependencies: Gson, JUnit 5, Mockito
Create initial classes: Student, Grade, GradeManager
Install cognitive-core (from M9); confirm /fitness runs
First commit on main: “Initial project structure”
Create branch: feature/core-logic
Verify: mvn compile works
Implement Student (name, ID, list of grades)
Implement GradeManager (add student, add grade, calculate average, save/load JSON)
Write JUnit tests — use Claude Code + write some by hand
Use TDD for at least one method
Run /test-scaffold to surface edge cases you missed
Commit iteratively: one feature at a time, meaningful messages
Verify: mvn test all green
Run /code-review — fix findings before you merge
Merge feature branch to main
Run mvn checkstyle:check pmd:check spotbugs:check
Fix all violations (AI help OK, but understand each fix)
Add JaCoCo — target 70%+ coverage
Write Jenkinsfile with stages: Build, Test, Quality, Package
Capture /fitness score (needed for the M11 bonus)
Final commit & push to GitHub: “Add CI pipeline and quality gates”
Verify: mvn clean package succeeds with zero warnings

title: Professional AI Usage: Signs & Red Flags
desc: What professional AI-assisted development looks like — and what it doesn’t. (Grading bonuses: see Module 11.)

items: Commits show back-and-forth: generate → review → fix → commit
CLAUDE.md evolves over the project (not written once and forgotten)
/fitness score improves across commits
Student can explain why they accepted or rejected AI suggestions
One giant commit with AI-generated everything
CLAUDE.md is a copy-paste template with no project-specific content
Student cannot explain code they committed
No evidence of quality tool usage between AI generations

title: Submission
desc: Solo project — push to GitHub by the announced deadline.

items: Create a GitHub repo (public, or private with instructor granted access)
Push all your commits — the git history is part of the grade
Final commit on main before the deadline
Submit the repo URL through the course channel
git log --oneline — iterative commits, not one big dump
CLAUDE.md + .claude/ — your rules and commands
mvn clean verify — must pass
For bonuses: /fitness screenshot, Architecture Checklist evidence — see Module 11 Grading Policy
